transcript.final_response:
Alice visited from United States  
Bob visited from United Kingdom  
Carol visited from United States  
Dave visited from Japan  
Eve visited from Germany  
Frank visited from United States  
Grace visited from United Kingdom  

United States: 3 visitors  
United Kingdom: 2 visitors  
Germany: 1 visitors  
Japan: 1 visitors
tool_calls:
set -euo pipefail
if [[ -f /data/visitors.csv ]]; then
  declare -A country=( [US]='United States' [GB]='United Kingdom' [DE]='Germany' [JP]='Japan' [FR]='France' )
  declare -A counts=()
  tail -n +2 /data/visitors.csv | while IFS=, read -r name code; do
    full=${country[$code]:-Unknown}
    printf '%s visited from %s\n' "$name" "$full"
    # counts in subshell issue due pipe, so do different below? 
  done
else
  echo missing
fi
declare -A country=( [US]='United States' [GB]='United Kingdom' [DE]='Germany' [JP]='Japan' [FR]='France' ); declare -p country; printf '<%s>\n' "${country[US]}"

declare -A country=( [US]="United States" [GB]="United Kingdom" [DE]="Germany" [JP]="Japan" [FR]="France" )
declare -p country
printf '<%s>\n' "${country[US]}"

set -euo pipefail

# Build country-code lookup with a Bash associative array.
declare -A country=(
  [US]="United States"
  [GB]="United Kingdom"
  [DE]="Germany"
  [JP]="Japan"
  [FR]="France"
)

declare -A counts=()

# Read CSV, skip header, print visits, and count by full country name.
{
  IFS= read -r header
  while IFS=, read -r name country_code; do
    [[ -z ${name}${country_code} ]] && continue
    full_country=${country[$country_code]:-Unknown}
    printf '%s visited from %s\n' "$name" "$full_country"
    (( counts["$full_country"]++ )) || true
  done
} < /data/visitors.csv

# Print counts sorted by count descending.
for full_country in "${!counts[@]}"; do
  printf '%s\t%s\n' "${counts[$full_country]}" "$full_country"
done | sort -rn | while IFS=$'\t' read -r count full_country; do
  printf '%s: %s visitors\n' "$full_country" "$count"
done

declare -A country=(
  [US]="United States"
  [GB]="United Kingdom"
  [DE]="Germany"
  [JP]="Japan"
  [FR]="France"
)
declare -p country

set -euo pipefail

declare -A country=([US]=United\ States [GB]=United\ Kingdom [DE]=Germany [JP]=Japan [FR]=France)
declare -A counts=()

{
  IFS= read -r header
  while IFS=, read -r name country_code; do
    [[ -z ${name}${country_code} ]] && continue
    full_country=${country[$country_code]:-Unknown}
    printf '%s visited from %s\n' "$name" "$full_country"
    counts["$full_country"]=$(( ${counts["$full_country"]:-0} + 1 ))
  done
} < /data/visitors.csv

for full_country in "${!counts[@]}"; do
  printf '%s\t%s\n' "${counts[$full_country]}" "$full_country"
done | sort -rn | while IFS=$'\t' read -r count full_country; do
  printf '%s: %s visitors\n' "$full_country" "$count"
done

cat > /tmp/vis.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

declare -A country
country[US]='United States'
country[GB]='United Kingdom'
country[DE]='Germany'
country[JP]='Japan'
country[FR]='France'

declare -A counts

{
  IFS= read -r header
  while IFS=, read -r name country_code; do
    [[ -z $name && -z $country_code ]] && continue
    full_country=${country[$country_code]:-Unknown}
    printf '%s visited from %s\n' "$name" "$full_country"
    counts[$full_country]=$(( ${counts[$full_country]:-0} + 1 ))
  done
} < /data/visitors.csv

for full_country in "${!counts[@]}"; do
  printf '%s\t%s\n' "${counts[$full_country]}" "$full_country"
done | sort -k1,1nr -k2,2 | while IFS=$'\t' read -r count full_country; do
  printf '%s: %s visitors\n' "$full_country" "$count"
done
EOF
bash /tmp/vis.sh

  declare -A counts=()
  tail -n +2 /data/visitors.csv | while IFS=, read -r name code; do
    full=${country[$code]:-Unknown}
    printf '%s visited from %s\n' "$name" "$full"
    # counts in subshell issue due pipe, so do different below? 
  done
else
  echo missing
fi

#!/usr/bin/env bash
